name: HPE ProLiant / Synergy servers (iLO-managed UEFI)
description: HPE enterprise servers (ProLiant Gen10+, Synergy, Apollo, Cray) ship UEFI firmware managed through the iLO 5 / iLO 6 BMC. CRTM_VERSION identifies the HPE firmware generation; iLO provides a separate attestation surface (iLO Security Attestation).
platforms: ProLiant DL Gen10 / DL Gen10 Plus / DL Gen11
secure-boot-default: disabled on server SKUs (can be enabled per-deployment)
silicon-root-of-trust: HPE iLO 5+ implements a Silicon Root of Trust (SRoT) anchored in the iLO chip's TrustZone, independent of Intel/AMD CPU fTPM. Measured-boot chain: iLO → UEFI → OS.
ek-root-ca-source: HPE Integrated Lights-Out (iLO) Security Attestation CA; downloadable from the HPE support portal per server model.
notes: HPE Silicon Root of Trust provides a lifecycle attestation for the iLO firmware itself, separate from the TPM-rooted attestation for UEFI + OS. A LapEE verifier wanting the full chain on HPE gear should query iLO for the SRoT cert + TPM for the UEFI/OS attestation.
source: HPE ProLiant Security Whitepaper (2023); observed PK cert patterns from HPE ProLiant test logs.
